MARGARET Crassus (-after 14 Jun 1247).  Her parentage and first marriage are confi …

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
NOTE: This file was reinstated Rodney Piper on the 14th of September 2023 and was attached to her rightful parents on this date and attached to MSMF-15R. All people have to do is their research here and locate it, it is not too hard to do. 

Margaret Marshal (1152-1244) was a noblewoman and the wife of Seigneur de Soulangy William Crassus who was one of the most famous knights in medieval England. She was born in Wales and was the daughter of John FitzGilbert, a powerful baron, and Sybilla de Sailsbury, who's ancestor is Edward of Sailsbury of Old Sailsbury Castle. He controlled estates in conjunction with Jean Martin, and was mentioned in the Domesday Book [see the following url https://opendomesday.org/place/TL0211/great-gaddesden/.] and [https://opendomesday.org/name/edward-of-salisbury/] who was lord over 28 townships and tenant in chief of 47 townships. 

Ralph married Margaret Crassus niece of William, Earl of Pembroke. The couple had three sons, and two daughters, Joan and Isabella, Ralph II, William Percival and Roger, who successively inherited the barony. Ralph, I died in 1210. [source Wikipedia with the addition of two daughter which were not mentioned.]

nameMeanings.name: Marshal
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Marshall  .

variants.name: Marshall
meanings: English: usually an occupational name ‘farrier’, occasionally a status name ‘chief official of a royal household or court; a high officer of state’, from Middle English mareshal and Old French maresc(h)al. An even wider range of meanings is found in some other languages: compare for example Polish Marszałek (see Marszalek ). This name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. It is also borne by Jews, presumably as an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret Marshal was born after 1147, in Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Wales, her father, John FitzGilbert, was 8894 and her mother, Sibyl de Salisbury, was 8873. She had at least 5 sons and 1 daughter with William III Crassus Lord of Chipping Sodbury. She died from 8 January 1243 to 7 January 1244, in Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Marshal(이)가 1147년 이후 웨일스 p브로케실르 p브로크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John FitzGilbert(은)는 8894세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sibyl de Salisbury(은)는 8873세였다. 그녀는 William III Crassus Lord of Chipping Sodbury(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1243년 1월 8일부터 1244년 1월 7일까지 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르에 묻혔다.
